props.message:
THE LONG DRIFT

A quieter sister-festival to the Drift. Things here are forgotten across more than one day, but eventually still go.

A few people stay anyway.

Carved into a sun-faded post:

'STILL WAITING.'

root.speaker: Festival-Goer Pyt
root.text:
*he sits on a barrel. He is wearing what was a festival hat a year ago. The food on his plate has been there for a while.*

Walker. Welcome to the festival. Such as it is.

I was supposed to meet someone here. They were supposed to come back the next day. The next day was a year ago.

home.speaker: Festival-Goer Pyt
home.text:
*he smiles. The smile is gentle.*

Not yet. Not yet, walker. The next breath. Maybe the breath after.

If I leave and they come back and I'm not here, they'll think I forgot. The Drift forgets. The Long Drift remembers a little. I remember a lot.

who.speaker: Festival-Goer Pyt
who.text:
A friend. Just a friend. The kind of friend you don't have many of.

I'm sure they're fine. The road is the road. They'll be back.

*he looks at the empty plate.*

bye.speaker: Festival-Goer Pyt
bye.text: Walk well. If you see anyone on the road who looks like they might be coming this way — point them at the post. The post says 'still waiting'. They'll know.
